reason: All three residual findings are minor and do not affect correctness or practical usability. The core content for each section is accurate and complete. Missing hints reduce search discoverability marginally, and the omitted footnote is a conceptual nuance that does not affect correct implementation decisions.

findings_assessment.location: s3 (自動インジェクション)
findings_assessment.justification: The missing footnote clarifies that loose coupling is a general DI container benefit, not exclusive to autowiring. This is a conceptual nuance. Developers can still configure and use autowiring correctly without it. No incorrect implementation would result from its absence—the primary use case (reducing property element configuration via autowiring) is fully covered.

The reference to `repository_import_override_priority` already appears in the s2 section body text, so users who reach s2 by any path will see it. The closely related `repository_import_dir_override_priority` is already present in s2 hints. The only consequence of the missing hint is reduced search discoverability for the specific label `repository_import_override_priority`, which is a secondary retrieval concern rather than a content gap.

The class names `HelloComponent`, `HelloMessageProvider`, and `BasicHelloMessageProvider` are example/illustration classes, not production API types. They already appear in s3 hints, so searches for these names find a relevant section. s4's functional hints (`ネストコンポーネント`, `component要素のネスト`, `コンポーネント名のドット結合`) cover the real use-case search paths. The gap is purely cosmetic hint completeness.
